tagline: Lose small. Win big. — Paper-traded portfolio. Hypothetical performance for educational purposes only.

stress_tests.description: Rapid 33.9% selloff in SPY over 23 trading days as COVID-19 spread.
Grinding 9-month decline as the Fed hiked rates aggressively.
Credit crisis bear market; SPY drew down 56.5% from Oct 2007 peak.
Tech bubble burst; SPY -49.1% peak-to-trough over 30 months.
Flash crash; -20.4% on a single trading day.
Yen carry unwind shock; SPY -8.4% in one week.

monte_carlo.method: Bootstrap of historical daily NAV returns. 10,000 paths, 21-trading-day horizon (~1 month).

factor_stress.description: Concentrated drawdown in Tech and Communication Services.
Long-duration growth and rate-sensitive sectors hit; banks benefit.
Oil shock — producers rally, downstream consumers pinched.
Cyclicals and credit-sensitive names down hard; defensives flat.
China-listed and China-revenue names take a 25% hit.
Growth and cyclicals rip; defensives lag.

correlation_tail_risk.method: Portfolio variance recomputed with all cross-correlations forced to 1.0, holding individual vols constant. 3σ 1-day P&L uses stressed annualized vol / √252.
